label: Delivery model
desc: Who is responsible for producing the outcome?
optDesc: You do the work; the outcome is delivered to the buyer.
You and the buyer produce the outcome together.
The buyer executes alone using what you give them.

label: Source of value
desc: What is the main thing the client is really paying for?
optDesc: Your personal time, skill, and judgment.
A repeatable process, method, or automation.
A tangible thing you've produced (content, IP, product).

label: Interaction timing
optDesc: Live, real-time (calls, sessions, events).
On each party's own schedule (messages, recordings).

label: Participation format
desc: How many people benefit from one unit of your effort?
optDesc: One buyer at a time — solo or private.
A group served together in the same session.
An open-ended community people join and leave.
One effort reaches unlimited buyers (async, at scale).

label: Touch intensity
desc: How much direct human attention does each client receive?
optDesc: Minimal contact; largely self-serve.
Periodic check-ins and support.
Frequent, hands-on, individualized attention.

label: Delivery medium
desc: What does the client interact with to receive the value?
optDesc: Your live time and attention (calls, coaching).
Materials you've produced (courses, templates, docs).
Software or a product the buyer operates.

label: Scope breadth
desc: How much of the client's journey are you responsible for?
optDesc: One specific result.
One whole area of their work or life.
The full journey from start to finish.

label: Scope depth
desc: How far into the problem do you go?
optDesc: The visible surface problem.
The layer beneath the symptom.
The underlying root cause.

label: System impact
desc: How much does the client's business actually change?
optDesc: Improves one part; the whole stays intact.
Reshapes a meaningful chunk of how they work.
Rebuilds how the whole thing works.

label: Buyer participation intensity
desc: How much does the client have to do for this to work?
optDesc: Receives the value with little effort of their own.
Contributes work at key points.
Must put in substantial, sustained work.

label: Effort duration
desc: How long does the commitment last?
optDesc: A single action and they're done.
Concentrated effort over a defined period.
Continuous effort with no fixed end.

label: Commitment reversibility
desc: How easy is it for the buyer to exit after saying yes?
optDesc: Fully committed once they're in.
Some lock-in; partial refund or wind-down.
Easy, low-cost exit at any time.

label: Pricing model
desc: What logic determines how the buyer pays?
optDesc: One set price for the offer.
Price flexes with tier, scope, or results.
Billed by time spent (hourly, daily, retainer).
A subscription that renews on a cycle.

label: Price framing
desc: What economic lens does the buyer use on the price?
optDesc: A cost to be minimized.
Capital deployed for a return.
Part cost, part investment.

label: Authority dynamic
desc: What role do you occupy in the buyer's decisions?
optDesc: You direct the process and call the shots.
You guide; the buyer decides alongside you.
You advise; the buyer directs and decides.
